topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion in the quiet hum of my evening. A profile picture loaded: a grainy, overly bright photo of a grinning face I hadn't seen in fifteen years. My breath hitched. It was Mark. Mark, the one who used to dismantle my confidence with a cruel flick of his wrist in the third grade. My fingers hovered over the 'Accept' button, trembling slightly. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling at my chest. Why now? What was he thinking? I quickly minimized the message, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. I spent the next hour pacing my small apartment, running a hand repeatedly through my hair, the silence of the room amplifying the frantic rhythm inside me.

[story 2]

I stared at the incoming request, my gaze snagging on the familiar, mocking smile in the profile picture. It felt like a physical blow, stealing the air from my lungs. Every muscle in my jaw clenched. *No. Absolutely not.* I felt a sudden, intense wave of nausea wash over me. I reread the request three times, searching for some hidden clue, some explanation that would make sense of this unwelcome reappearance. My hands started shaking so badly I had to grip the edge of the desk to steady them. I slid the phone face down on the table, needing distance, needing to put physical space between myself and the unsettling digital tether. The silence that followed was heavy, suffocating.

[story 3]

When the notification popped up, I froze mid-sip of my tea. The steam curled around my face, but I couldn't feel the warmth. It was him. The memory of his sneering face from years ago flooded my mind, sharp and unwelcome. A deep chill ran down my spine, making the fine hairs on my arms stand up. I slowly set the mug down, the clink of ceramic sounding deafening in the quiet room. My throat felt tight, constricted, as if I couldn't draw a full breath. I kept glancing at the screen, unable to look away, a strange mixture of dread and sheer bewilderment swirling beneath the surface of my calm facade.
